content.full_text:
CITY OF Riverside CITY HALL • 3900 Main Street • Riverside, California 92522 • 714/782-5991

MAYOR
TERRY FRIZZEL

CITY COUNCIL

RONALD LOVERIDGE
Ward No. 1

JACK CLARKE
Ward No. 2

JEAN MANSFIELD
Ward No. 3

BOB BUSTER
Ward No. 4

GEORGE OSBORNE
Ward No. 5

FRED THOMPSON
Ward No. 6

LAURA PEARSON
Ward No. 7

May 21, 1991

Mr. John Wallace
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Wallace:

As per our conversation on February 15th, regarding the use of campaign funds for a newsletter. I am requesting written verification of the information you gave me during this telephone conversation

My understanding is that my bank account funds from my last campaign may be used for a newsletter to my constituents. This cannot be done after the 3 month period prior to the November 5th election. Please give me the exact date that I can no longer use these funds in this manner.

I understand that I may use no less than 6 point type on the outside of the envelope and must be in a color and print that is legible. I also must include Councilmember Laura Pearson, my campaign headquarters address and phone number. Does this also pertain to this information appearing on the inside of the newsletter, as well? My understanding was that nowhere on this newsletter may I refer to the upcoming election. On all other campaign materials, the name of my treasurer was required, do I need to include him on this mailer?

Do I understand you correctly, that I must amend my "Statement of Organization" by filing a 502 & 410 and that I may use my same political ID number? Please forward these forms to me.

I understood you to say that I may rollover the remaining funds from the previous campaign into my new campaign account.

Mr. John Wallace
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
March 10, 1991
Page 2


Please advise if my understanding of our conversation is
correct or have I missed something that may come back to
haunt me.

Thank you, in advance, for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Laura Pearson
City Councilmember
Ward 1

I understand that I may use no less than 6 point type on the outside of the envelope and must be in a color and print that is legible. I also must include Councilmember Laura Pearson, my campaign headquarters address and phone number. Does this also pertain to this information appearing on the inside of the newsletter, as well? My understanding was that nowhere on this newsletter may I refer to the upcoming election. On all other campaign materials, the name of my treasurer was required, do I need to include him on this mailer? Do I understand you correctly, that I must amend my "Statement of Organization" by filing a 502 & 410 and that I may use my same political ID number? Please forward these forms to me. I understood you to say that I may rollover the remaining funds from the previous campaign into my new campaign account.
sections.question_synthetic: A city councilmember seeks written clarification on multiple matters regarding the use of leftover campaign funds for a newsletter, including timing restrictions, disclosure requirements on the envelope and newsletter content, and procedures for amending campaign finance disclosures.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a local official to FPPC, not an FPPC response. The document contains multiple specific questions about campaign finance rules but lacks the structure of an official FPPC advice letter. Date discrepancy noted: letter header shows May 21, 1991 but body shows March 10, 1991. No formal CONCLUSION or ADVICE section from FPPC is present.

embedding.summary: A Riverside city councilmember (Ward 2) writes to the FPPC requesting written verification of telephone advice provided by Mr. John Wallace on February 15, 1991, concerning the permissible use of remaining campaign funds for a newsletter, disclosure requirements, and campaign finance filing procedures.
